d4b_e1_a.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e1_a.text: Day 4 and your team is running on empty. Low morale has become visible to customers and it is starting to affect everything. This needs urgent attention.

d4b_e1_b.speaker: Staff
d4b_e1_b.text: I need to be honest with you. Two team members are thinking of quitting. The pressure and low morale have become too much. We need something to change today.

choices.text: Have an immediate one-on-one with each person
Offer immediate pay increase to retain them
Tell them you understand if they need to leave

d4b_e1_c_oneonone.speaker: Staff
d4b_e1_c_oneonone.text: Just having you sit down and listen made a huge difference. I feel heard for the first time in weeks. I am willing to give it another chance.

d4b_e1_c_pay.speaker: Staff
d4b_e1_c_pay.text: The pay increase helps but honestly it is not just about money. We hope things change because the financial fix cannot be the only solution.

d4b_e1_c_restructure.speaker: Staff
d4b_e1_c_restructure.text: Changing the structure actually makes a real difference. The pressure points were specific and you addressed them directly. Thank you for listening.

d4b_e1_c_leave.speaker: Staff
d4b_e1_c_leave.text: You actually mean that? It is hard to hear but also kind of a relief. One of them decided to stay. The other is putting in notice tomorrow.

d4b_e1_result_oneonone.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e1_result_oneonone.text: Direct personal attention is the most powerful retention tool available to a small business leader. People leave managers not companies.

d4b_e1_result_pay.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e1_result_pay.text: Compensation addresses symptoms but rarely the root cause of burnout. Use the time it buys to fix the underlying culture and workload issues.

d4b_e1_result_restructure.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e1_result_restructure.text: Addressing root causes rather than symptoms is exceptional leadership. Burnout is usually a systems problem not a people problem.

d4b_e1_result_leave.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e1_result_leave.text: Graceful exits preserve dignity and relationships. The one who stayed chose to — that voluntary commitment is more valuable than reluctant presence.

d4b_e2_a.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e2_a.text: Burnout does not stay internal — it leaks into customer experience. A customer left a detailed review describing staff as disengaged and robotic.

d4b_e2_b.speaker: You
d4b_e2_b.text: The review is spreading. It describes exactly what I feared — that our internal struggles are now visible to customers. I need to act on both fronts.

choices.text: Respond to the review and fix the team issue simultaneously
Fix the team first — customers come second today
Address the public review first — protect the brand
Bring in a consultant to assess the situation

d4b_e2_c_both.speaker: Staff
d4b_e2_c_both.text: You are juggling a lot right now but the fact that you are addressing us AND the public issue simultaneously shows real commitment to solving this properly.

d4b_e2_c_team.speaker: Staff
d4b_e2_c_team.text: Choosing us over the public image means everything. We will work twice as hard knowing you prioritize your people over appearances.

d4b_e2_c_review.speaker: Customer Online
d4b_e2_c_review.text: I saw your professional response to my review. It was thoughtful and genuine. I hope things improve internally too — the product is still great.

d4b_e2_c_consult.speaker: Consultant
d4b_e2_c_consult.text: After reviewing the situation the core issue is unclear role expectations and insufficient recognition systems. Both are fixable within a week.

d4b_e2_result_both.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e2_result_both.text: Managing internal and external crises simultaneously is exhausting but necessary. Great leaders do not get to choose which fire to fight — they manage all of them.

d4b_e2_result_team.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e2_result_team.text: People first leadership has a cost in the short term but builds the kind of loyalty that recovers reputations faster than any PR strategy.

d4b_e2_result_review.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e2_result_review.text: Brand protection buys time but is not a solution. Use the goodwill you rebuild externally to create space for genuine internal change.

d4b_e2_result_consult.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e2_result_consult.text: External perspective on internal problems can break the blind spots that develop when you are too close to a situation. Asking for help is wisdom.

d4b_e3_a.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e3_a.text: Despite everything today was a turning point. End of day and you have a chance to set a new standard — a reset moment for the whole team.

d4b_e3_b.speaker: Staff
d4b_e3_b.text: Things felt different today. Difficult but different. Like we are finally being honest about what needs to change. What happens from here boss?

choices.text: Announce a formal recovery plan with clear milestones
Declare today a reset and start fresh tomorrow
Ask the team what they need to move forward
Acknowledge what went wrong and commit to doing better

d4b_e3_c_plan.speaker: Staff
d4b_e3_c_plan.text: A real plan with real milestones. Finally something concrete to work toward. The team is cautiously optimistic for the first time in days.

d4b_e3_c_reset.speaker: Staff
d4b_e3_c_reset.text: A clean slate. Sometimes that is exactly what is needed. The symbolism of a genuine reset matters almost as much as the practical changes.

d4b_e3_c_ask.speaker: Staff
d4b_e3_c_ask.text: You are asking us? That alone changes everything. We have ideas we have been sitting on for weeks. Thank you for finally opening the floor.

d4b_e3_c_acknowledge.speaker: Staff
d4b_e3_c_acknowledge.text: Hearing you acknowledge what went wrong means more than any bonus or plan. We needed to know you saw it too. That changes everything.

d4b_e3_result_plan.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e3_result_plan.text: Recovery plans with clear measurable milestones convert vague hope into trackable progress. Teams need to see the path not just hear about the destination.

d4b_e3_result_reset.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e3_result_reset.text: Symbolic resets matter in team culture. The ritual of declaring a fresh start releases accumulated tension and creates permission to behave differently.

d4b_e3_result_ask.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e3_result_ask.text: The answers to most business problems already exist within your team. Leaders who ask unlock that wisdom. Leaders who do not keep solving the same problems alone.

d4b_e3_result_acknowledge.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e3_result_acknowledge.text: Accountability without defensiveness is rare leadership. When people see their leader own mistakes it gives them permission to be honest too.
